prompt: Dispute resolution optional competency Level 2 evidence might include:

options.text: Starting fights on site
Supporting adjudication/negotiation with documented quantum substantiation
Withholding all records from adjudicator
Ignoring contract notice requirements

explanation: Dispute resolution optional covers supported participation in ADR processes.

distractorExplanations.a: Dispute resolution is formal professional process.
distractorExplanations.c: Good faith dispute resolution requires evidence.

prompt: RICS consumer service standards apply when:

options.text: Never — QS is exempt
Only residential leasehold work always
Providing regulated services to external clients under RICS regulation
Only when client requests them in writing

explanation: RICS regulated practices must meet consumer service standards.

distractorExplanations.a: RICS regulated firms follow consumer service standards.
distractorExplanations.b: Standards apply broadly to regulated services.
distractorExplanations.d: Standards apply by regulation, not opt-in.

prompt: Personal development mandatory competency Level 1 means:

options.text: No learning after university degree
CPD only watching unrelated TV
Forging CPD records to meet quota
Maintaining CPD and reflective learning to improve professional practice

explanation: Personal development Level 1 is ongoing relevant CPD and reflection.

distractorExplanations.b: CPD must be relevant structured learning.

prompt: Consultancy QS asked to provide 'lowest possible' cost plan to help client secure funding knowing it is optimistic. Ethics?

options.text: Present realistic range with assumptions and risks — not misleading single optimistic figure
Provide unrealistically low figure as requested
Refuse all cost planning forever
Hide assumptions in footnote only

explanation: Cost advice must not mislead funders; assumptions and risks must be transparent.

distractorExplanations.b: Misleading cost advice breaches integrity and client care.
distractorExplanations.c: Ethical path is realistic transparent advice.
distractorExplanations.d: Material assumptions must be clear to client.

prompt: Team leadership mandatory competency Level 1 for assistant QS includes:

options.text: Refusing all collaboration
Supporting team delivery, seeking guidance, and contributing reliably to shared outputs
Taking credit for all seniors' work
Ignoring firm quality procedures

explanation: Team leadership Level 1 is reliable collaborative contribution within teams.

distractorExplanations.a: Teamwork is part of professional delivery.
distractorExplanations.c: Honest attribution is part of integrity.
distractorExplanations.d: Team delivery follows firm QA processes.

prompt: Business planning mandatory Level 1 for employed QS means:

options.text: Writing firm's five-year M&A strategy alone as graduate
Ignoring commercial context of assignments
Understanding how your work contributes to departmental and firm objectives
Only focusing on billable hours fraudulently inflated

explanation: Business planning Level 1 is awareness of how role supports firm goals.

distractorExplanations.a: Level 1 is awareness of business context, not sole strategic ownership.
distractorExplanations.b: Business planning awareness includes understanding firm objectives.
distractorExplanations.d: Fraudulent time recording breaches conduct rules.

prompt: Confidential employer budget data on laptop left on train. Immediate action?

options.text: Hope no one finds it
Blame client for giving data
Post budget on LinkedIn for transparency
Report breach per firm GDPR/security policy; notify client if required; mitigate harm

explanation: Data breaches require immediate policy-compliant reporting and mitigation.

distractorExplanations.a: Data breaches require immediate reporting and mitigation.
distractorExplanations.b: QS firm holds data protection responsibilities.

prompt: Facilities management optional competency link to QS is:

options.text: Understanding how construction decisions affect operational costs and asset performance
Irrelevant to all QS roles
Only cleaning schedules
Replacing cost plan with FM invoice only

explanation: FM optional links construction cost decisions to lifecycle operational outcomes.

distractorExplanations.b: FM optional connects built asset cost to operations.
distractorExplanations.c: FM optional is broader operational asset management.

prompt: RICS disciplinary matter for dishonest measurement could result in:

options.text: Automatic promotion
Sanctions up to expulsion from RICS and reputational damage
No consequences ever
Mandatory APC pass

explanation: RICS enforces conduct rules through disciplinary sanctions including expulsion.

distractorExplanations.a: Misconduct leads to sanctions, not reward.
distractorExplanations.c: RICS enforces conduct through disciplinary process.
distractorExplanations.d: Disciplinary outcomes are separate from assessment success.

prompt: Inclusive communication with neurodiverse client stakeholder means:

options.text: Refuse to meet neurodiverse clients
Use maximum technical jargon to impress
Clear written summaries, defined acronyms, and check understanding respectfully
Record meetings without consent

explanation: Inclusive communication adapts format and clarity to audience needs.

distractorExplanations.a: Inclusive practice adapts communication, not exclusion.
distractorExplanations.d: Recording requires consent and policy compliance.

prompt: Anti-money laundering client due diligence for new developer client requires:

options.text: No checks for friendly referrals
Accept cash payments without questions
Delegate entirely to client self-certify without verification
Identity verification and risk-based source-of-funds checks per MLR regulations

explanation: AML regulations require identity and risk-based due diligence on clients.

distractorExplanations.a: AML checks apply regardless of referral source.
distractorExplanations.b: AML requires source of funds scrutiny where applicable.
distractorExplanations.c: Firms must perform regulated AML checks.

prompt: Level 2 optional Contract administration evidence example:

options.text: Issuing compliant payment notices and tracking compensation events under NEC on live project
Never attending contract meetings
Only reading contracts without application
Altering signed contract unilaterally

explanation: Contract administration optional is evidenced through live contract process tasks.

distractorExplanations.b: Administration requires active contract process management.
distractorExplanations.d: Contract changes require proper variation procedure.

prompt: Professional scepticism when reviewing contractor claim means:

options.text: Approve all claims to avoid conflict
Test substantiation against contract and records before certifying
Reject all claims automatically
Certify based on friendship

explanation: Professional scepticism balances fair evaluation of substantiated claims.

distractorExplanations.a: Scepticism protects client and professional integrity.
distractorExplanations.c: Valid substantiated claims must be paid.

prompt: RICS global professional statement on ethics requires members to:

options.text: Pursue fee income above all else
Discriminate when profitable
Act honourably, avoid conflicts, and uphold public trust in the profession
Hide mistakes permanently

explanation: RICS ethics framework prioritises public trust, integrity, and conflict avoidance.

distractorExplanations.a: Public trust and integrity supersede commercial gain.
distractorExplanations.d: Integrity requires acknowledging and correcting errors.

prompt: Research analysis and problem solving mandatory Level 2 means:

options.text: Guessing solutions without data
Copying answers from internet without verification
Avoiding difficult problems entirely
Structured analysis of complex cost problems with evidence-based recommendations

explanation: Research and problem solving Level 2 uses structured evidence-based analysis.

distractorExplanations.b: Professional analysis verifies sources and logic.
distractorExplanations.c: Competency is demonstrated by tackling complex problems.

prompt: When optional competency overlaps core competency evidence, APC guidance says:

options.text: Use distinct examples or clearly separate aspects to avoid double-counting same example
Submit identical paragraph for all competencies
Ignore optional competencies entirely
Fabricate extra projects

explanation: Distinct evidence prevents double-counting across core and optional competencies.

distractorExplanations.b: Assessors require distinct evidence per competency.

prompt: Client requests QS to pressure subcontractor for unlawful payment deduction. Response?

options.text: Threaten subcontractor illegally
Refuse; advise on lawful set-off and contract remedies only
Deduct without notice or contract right
Accept kickback for compliance

explanation: QS must refuse unlawful payment practices and advise on lawful remedies.

distractorExplanations.a: Unlawful pressure breaches conduct and law.

prompt: Continuing professional development for ethics should include:

options.text: Only technical measurement with no ethics content
Falsified attendance certificates
RICS ethics modules, case studies, and firm policy updates on conduct and AML
No CPD because already chartered eventually

explanation: Ethics CPD includes RICS modules and firm conduct policy training.

distractorExplanations.a: Ethics CPD is required component of professional development.
distractorExplanations.d: CPD continues throughout career pre and post chartership.

prompt: Programming optional — delay analysis interface with QS means:

options.text: Ignoring time impact of variations
Only drawing bar charts without cost link
Automatic 10% claim for any rain
Quantifying prolongation costs linked to programme slippage and causation

explanation: Programming optional for QS includes prolongation cost linkage to programme.

distractorExplanations.a: Delay analysis connects time and cost claims.
distractorExplanations.b: QS programming optional links schedule to cost consequences.

prompt: Acting as expert witness in quantum dispute requires:

options.text: Independent objective opinion within area of expertise, documented and defensible
Advocating client's position regardless of facts
Withholding adverse data
Accepting instructions to mislead

explanation: Expert witnesses owe independent objective duty with defensible opinions.

distractorExplanations.b: Expert duty is to the tribunal/court, not partisan advocacy.
distractorExplanations.c: Experts must be objective and complete.
distractorExplanations.d: Misleading expert evidence is serious misconduct.

prompt: Mandatory competency Legal/regulatory compliance Level 2 for QS includes:

options.text: Practising as solicitor without qualification
Applying CDM, construction act payment rules, and relevant contract law basics in advice
Ignoring HGCRA payment requirements
Breaching GDPR to share client data

explanation: Legal/regulatory Level 2 is applying relevant construction law in QS practice.

distractorExplanations.a: QS applies relevant law; complex matters refer to lawyers.
distractorExplanations.c: Payment legislation is core compliance knowledge.
distractorExplanations.d: Data protection is legal compliance requirement.

prompt: Social value in procurement advice from QS might include:

options.text: Ignoring client ESG policies
Treating social value as free text only
Costing community employment targets, local spend, and SME participation in tender evaluation
Guaranteeing social outcomes without contract mechanism

explanation: QS can support social value evaluation integration in procurement.

distractorExplanations.a: Social value increasingly forms procurement criteria.
distractorExplanations.b: Social value can be weighted and costed in evaluation.
distractorExplanations.d: Advice should align social value with enforceable mechanisms.

prompt: Final assessment interview starts at Level 3 and may probe down to Level 1. Preparation requires:

options.text: Only Level 1 definitions memorised
Refusing Level 1 questions as beneath you
No preparation because interview is informal chat
Deep Level 3 examples ready with underpinning Level 1-2 knowledge for follow-up questions

explanation: Interview probes Level 3 first with potential descent to Level 1 knowledge checks.

distractorExplanations.a: Interview starts at Level 3 competency depth.
distractorExplanations.b: Assessors probe down when Level 3 answers are weak.
distractorExplanations.c: Final assessment is formal competency examination.

prompt: Managing director asks to hide subcontractor insolvency from public sector client. Ethics?

options.text: Disclose material risk to client promptly; confidentiality does not cover concealing material project risk
Conceal to protect firm share price only
Blame client for choosing subcontractor
Fabricate alternative subcontractor name

explanation: Material delivery risks like insolvency must be disclosed to the client.

distractorExplanations.b: Material project risks must be disclosed to client.
distractorExplanations.c: Professional duty is transparent risk communication.

prompt: Optional competency Taxation allowances and reliefs at Level 2 might support:

options.text: Evading tax illegally
Client decisions on land remediation relief, capital allowances, or VAT treatment with specialist input
Replacing qualified tax advisers on all matters
Ignoring VAT on international supplies

explanation: Taxation optional supports lawful reliefs with appropriate specialist collaboration.

distractorExplanations.a: Competency covers lawful reliefs, not evasion.
distractorExplanations.c: QS supports with specialist tax advice where needed.
distractorExplanations.d: VAT treatment is relevant commercial consideration.
